desc:
Make the engine account for its own state — append-only ledger, head-authoritative orient

## Intent
The engine that compiles agent state cannot account for its own. During fleet r238 (2026-06-07) the completion ledger froze at r235 across three committed rounds, and fleet orient floods its batch with 154 phantom 'init' nodes. The diagnosis was verified AND falsified by a two-agent fan-out before any code was touched. This round makes the ledger remember and makes orient trust head.json.

## Scenario
given a repo whose head.json names the current DAG and whose heads/ holds dozens of stale round DAGs,
when an agent completes nodes under parallel /roadmap-auto and then runs orient,
then every completion is durably recorded (no clobber, no cross-round collision) and orient returns head.json's DAG with a non-null dagId and its true frontier — not a flood of phantoms.

## Round
Round 6 · falsifier: a fixture with a head.json + 50 stale heads and 20 concurrent completions survives collision, flood, and clobber (v-self-host all-green). Carriers from the fleet r238 issue: BUG-B (ledger freeze), BUG-A (orient flood), symptom-3 (trail enrichment, non-bug). No prior engine round opened this falsifier; this round opens and closes it.

## Authority map
| domain | directories | allowed | forbidden |
|--------|-------------|---------|-----------|
| ledger | src/runtime/completion.ts · src/lib/ledger-migrate.ts | jsonl store, fold, migration helper | orient/advance edits, external-repo writes |
| orient | src/cli/orient.ts · src/runtime/fleet.ts · src/lib/heads-sweep.ts | head authority, dagId assert, heads sweep | ledger record shape, advance gate |
| cli | src/cli/advance.ts · bin/roadmap.ts | trail fields, fail-loud, command dispatch | completion internals, orient traversal |
| verify | test/ledger-self-host.mjs | fixture harness, assertions | production src/ edits |
| housekeeping | src/runtime/completion.ts (removal) · CLAUDE.md · .gitignore | delete dead paths, doctrine sync | behavior changes, new features |

## Stance pointers
Project CLAUDE.md · Layered Architecture (src/runtime is the IO boundary) · .roadmap/ File Topology (completed.json → becomes completed.jsonl) · §Verdict-ladder. User-global §CODE rules 5 (delete completely) + 8 (subtract before adding). The append-only discipline mirrors trail.jsonl and round receipts already in the repo.

## Subtraction budget
| counted | excluded | target |
|---------|----------|--------|
| src/runtime/completion.ts | *.test.ts · fixtures | the whole-array re-serialize path + the legacy non-evidence saveCompletion/loadCompletions duplication removed (≥ 25 LOC net) |
This round is NOT greenfield-exempt: it replaces a corruption-prone surface, so the old surface must leave. x-drop-array-path is the removal node; t-review asserts the dead symbols are gone.

## Test profile
| level | path glob | machine | max dur |
|-------|-----------|---------|---------|
| unit | src/**/*.test.ts | any | 30s |
| integration | test/ledger-self-host.mjs | any | 2min |
Validators run via pnpm vitest run <file> (unit) and node test/ledger-self-host.mjs (integration). pnpm run check = tsc --noEmit; pnpm run build = tsc+esbuild; pnpm test = full vitest suite. (Note: the repo's check:dag:define script references a nonexistent 'validate' command and is pre-broken — terminal does not depend on it.)

## Substrate state at round boundary
Engine repo (NOT the fleet model/canon domain — that inventory is N/A here). Verified by direct read + live orient + heads count on 2026-06-07:
- src/runtime/completion.ts — CONSUMES then SUPERSEDES: saveCompletionWithEvidence (l.158) whole-array atomicWriteJson keyed by bare nodeId; loadCompletionsWithEvidence (l.135); legacy saveCompletion/loadCompletions (l.224-276) now redundant. Record shape {nodeId,completedAt,dagId,validationChecks,validatorResults,gitSha,treeSha,branch} PRESERVED.
- src/cli/orient.ts — runFleetOrient (l.26-221): dagFiles from rc.activeDAGs (l.69-76), head.json fallback only when empty (l.79), dagId null when dagFiles.length!==1 (l.133). CONSUMES; this round modifies authority.
- src/runtime/fleet.ts — scanActiveDAGs (l.38) active-unless-_lineage.completedAt. CONSUMES; heads-sweep stamps completedAt.
- src/cli/advance.ts — advanceNode gate (l.71-79) flat 'not in current batch'; saveCompletionWithEvidence call (l.299); trail append (l.438) lacks top-level node/ok. CONSUMES; modified.
- bin/roadmap.ts — command switch (l.144-163). CONSUMES; two cases added (migrate-ledger, sweep-heads).
- NET-NEW: src/lib/ledger-migrate.ts, src/lib/heads-sweep.ts, test/ledger-self-host.mjs — justified: no extant substrate performs jsonl migration, head-completeness sweep, or self-host falsification.
- Predecessor: roadmap-r5-viewer-idle complete (12/12, chainReady). r6 is its successor. Prior heads under .roadmap/heads/ (6 files); no carriers from r5 (orthogonal concern).

## SCOPE FENCE
Engine code in /home/griffin/src/.dev/roadmap ONLY. Ships an idempotent migration helper + heads-sweep command but does NOT run them against /home/griffin/src/fleet or any external repo, and does NOT git rm fleet's completed.json or rewrite external history. The fleet-side data migration is the operator's to run later with the tools this round delivers.

## Evidence pinned from the verify×falsify fan-out
BUG-B mechanism is CLOBBER + COLLISION, not the issue's 'dagId-null append guard' (FALSIFIED — saveCompletionWithEvidence writes regardless of dagId). 71 post-freeze advances passed validators and reached the save yet produced ZERO net new completions; 28 collided on bare nodeId across rounds. BUG-A: repos[0] dagId:null, batch of 158 with 'init'×154. Symptom-3: trail keys ABSENT not null — enrichment, not repair.

init.desc:
Ratify the ledger-integrity bet · append-only jsonl · head authority · prune · trail · fail-loud

Produce the ratification receipt downstream nodes consume. Pin the policy: (a) completions persist append-only to .roadmap/completed.jsonl, one \n-terminated JSON object per line, record shape unchanged; (b) readers fold by (dagId, nodeId) keeping the LAST line; (c) loadCompletionsWithEvidence reads completed.jsonl when present, falls back to legacy completed.json array otherwise; (d) the migration helper is idempotent and NEVER deletes completed.json (git rm is the operator's act, out of scope); (e) fleet orient treats head.json.id as the single authoritative active DAG when head.json exists; (f) heads sweep stamps _lineage.completedAt only when every node of a head has a passing receipt; (g) advance trail row gains top-level node + ok; (h) advance fails loud (BATCH_DRIFT) when a node is in head.json.nodes but unplaceable; (i) SCOPE FENCE — engine repo only. Without this receipt downstream nodes have no anchor.

m-jsonl-store.desc:
Replace the whole-array completed.json rewrite with an append-only completed.jsonl store folded by (dagId, nodeId)

In src/runtime/completion.ts: (1) saveCompletionWithEvidence writes the record via fs.appendFileSync('.roadmap/completed.jsonl', JSON.stringify(record)+'\n') — NO read-modify-write of a whole array. (2) loadCompletionsWithEvidence reads completed.jsonl when it exists: split on \n, filter empty, JSON.parse per line, fold into a Map keyed by `${dagId ?? ''}::${nodeId}` keeping the LAST occurrence — the fix for cross-round node-id collision. When completed.jsonl is absent, fall back to the legacy completed.json array. (3) CompletionStore.hasPassing(nodeId)/filterByDagId(dagId) still resolve correctly: filterByDagId narrows to records whose dagId === arg OR undefined, within which nodeId is unique. Record shape UNCHANGED. Pair src/runtime/completion.test.ts proving the falsifiers in sidecar.test_cases (unit · refactor claim → unit tests).

x-drop-array-path.desc:
Remove the dead whole-array rewrite surface and the redundant legacy completion path

SUBTRACTION node. Once m-jsonl-store lands the append-only path, the whole-array re-serialize (atomicWriteJson of Array.from(completions.values()) in the save path) and the legacy non-evidence saveCompletion/loadCompletions pair (src/runtime/completion.ts l.224-276) are dead weight — callers use the evidence variants. Delete them completely (user-global rule 5: no 'removed' comments, no _-prefixed stubs). Update any remaining caller to the evidence path. This is the §Round-must-remove discipline: the corruption-prone surface must leave, not merely be bypassed. Receipt records the removed symbol names + line ranges for forensic reconstruction.

validate.command: cd /home/griffin/src/.dev/roadmap && pnpm run check && pnpm run build

m-migrate-ledger.desc:
Ship an idempotent migration helper + CLI converting completed.json → completed.jsonl without deleting the source

New file src/lib/ledger-migrate.ts exporting migrateCompletedJsonToJsonl(repoRoot): { migrated:boolean, count:number, reason?:string }. If .roadmap/completed.jsonl exists → {migrated:false, reason:'jsonl-exists'} (idempotent). Else if completed.json parses to an array → write each entry as a \n-terminated line and return {migrated:true, count}. Else → {migrated:false, reason:'no-source'}. MUST NOT delete or git rm completed.json. Wire `case 'migrate-ledger'` into bin/roadmap.ts emitting the result JSON. Pair src/lib/ledger-migrate.test.ts: fresh migration, idempotent re-run, no-source, and completed.json still present post-migration.

o-head-authority.desc:
Make fleet orient treat head.json as the authoritative active DAG, and assert dagId non-null when a head exists

In src/cli/orient.ts runFleetOrient: when a repo has .roadmap/head.json, build dagFiles from that single head (dagId = head.id) INSTEAD of expanding rc.activeDAGs into the batch. Stale heads must no longer flood repoBatch or null the dagId: if head.json exists, dagFiles=[{dagPath:head.json, dagId:head.id}] and repos[].dagId resolves non-null. Only when head.json is absent do we fall back to scanning heads/. Additionally, when head.json is present but the computed repos[].dagId is null, push a blocker ('null dagId with live head.json in <repo> — batch source has drifted'). Restores the head-as-source-of-truth invariant symptom-1 violated.

validate.command: cd /home/griffin/src/.dev/roadmap && pnpm run check && pnpm run build

o-prune-sweep.desc:
Add a heads sweep that marks fully-complete heads done so scanActiveDAGs stops returning phantoms

New file src/lib/heads-sweep.ts exporting sweepCompletedHeads(repoRoot): { swept:string[], scanned:number }. For each .roadmap/heads/*.json without _lineage.completedAt, load the repo's completion store and test whether EVERY node id of that head has a passing receipt (filterByDagId(head.id).hasPassing for all nodes). If so, stamp _lineage.completedAt=now and add its dagId to swept. Idempotent: a head already carrying completedAt is skipped. Wire `case 'sweep-heads'` into bin/roadmap.ts emitting JSON. Pair src/runtime/fleet.test.ts proving scanActiveDAGs shrinks after a sweep on a fixture where one head is fully complete and one is not. This is the PRUNE half of the user's 'both' scope; o-head-authority is the AUTHORITY half.

t-trail-fields.desc:
Emit top-level node + ok on the advance trail row (the operator's grep expectation)

In src/cli/advance.ts, the advance trail append (currently {ts,cmd,note,repo,position,level,detail:{completed,checks,passed}}) gains two top-level fields: node (the completed node id, = detail.completed) and ok (boolean, = detail.passed). Additive — existing fields stay. Satisfies symptom-3's grep expectation (jq '.node, .ok') without changing the trail's meaning. Pair a test asserting an advance writes a trail row whose top-level node and ok are populated (not absent).

h-advance-failloud.desc:
Fail loud when an advanced node is in head.json but unplaceable — distinguish stale-batch from not-ready

In src/cli/advance.ts advanceNode, the current gate rejects with a flat 'Node X is not in current batch'. Refine: when nodeId IS a key of dag.nodes but is neither in pos.position nor pos.batchRemaining, return a DISTINCT loud error — code BATCH_DRIFT, message 'Node <id> exists in head.json but the batch model cannot place it — the batch source has drifted from head.json; re-run orient/make', with a fix hint. When nodeId is genuinely not in dag.nodes, keep the existing 'Node not found' path. Makes symptom-1's signature self-announcing. Pair a test for both branches.

validate.command: cd /home/griffin/src/.dev/roadmap && pnpm run check && pnpm run build

d-doctrine-sync.desc:
Sync CLAUDE.md and .gitignore to the new ledger reality

Update the project CLAUDE.md '.roadmap/ File Topology' block: completed.json → completed.jsonl (append-only · jsonl · gitignored), and the CLI block: add migrate-ledger and sweep-heads commands. Add .roadmap/completed.jsonl to .gitignore (sibling to the existing completed.json line). Doctrine is part of the substrate (§Audit-must-test sibling: the map must not drift from the territory). Receipt records the edited anchors.

v-self-host.desc:
Self-host gate: a fixture proves collision, flood, and clobber are all defeated

New file test/ledger-self-host.mjs — a node script building throwaway temp fixtures and asserting three phases, writing pass flags to .roadmap/round-r6/v-self-host.json: (P1) COLLISION — append two completion records with the same nodeId under different dagId to a fixture completed.jsonl, load via the built engine's reader, assert BOTH survive (folded by (dagId,nodeId)). (P2) FLOOD — construct a fixture repo with a head.json (one DAG, known frontier) plus 50 stale heads/*.json lacking _lineage.completedAt, run the built roadmap orient with fleet authority, assert repos[].dagId === the head's id (non-null) and the batch === the head's frontier — NOT 51 phantoms. (P3) CLOBBER — spawn N=20 concurrent appends to a fixture completed.jsonl, assert all 20 lines land. Exits non-zero if any phase fails. This is the load-bearing falsifier (integration · behavioral claim → integration test).

t-review.desc:
Terminal falsifier: full suite green, type-check clean, DAG-define clean, dead surface confirmed gone, scope fence held

Final convergence gate consuming the self-host result. Assert: pnpm run check (tsc --noEmit), pnpm test (full vitest suite — no regressions elsewhere in the engine), and pnpm run build all pass; the whole-array rewrite and legacy saveCompletion are GONE from src/runtime/completion.ts (subtraction budget met); and no file outside /home/griffin/src/.dev/roadmap was modified (scope fence). Receipt records build presence, the removed-symbol confirmation, and a one-line summary of the three landings (ledger jsonl, orient authority+prune, trail+fail-loud).

validate.command: cd /home/griffin/src/.dev/roadmap && test $(grep -c 'function saveCompletion(' src/runtime/completion.ts) -eq 0 && test $(grep -c 'Array.from(completions.values())' src/runtime/completion.ts) -eq 0
cd /home/griffin/src/.dev/roadmap && pnpm run check && pnpm run build
